gua sering mikir, dan anehnya makin dipikir makin masuk akal, kalau sebenernya ini tuh dunia gua. dunia gua yang gua jalanin tiap hari, dunia gua yang gua bangunin pas bangun tidur, dunia gua yang gua rasain capeknya, senengnya, kosongnya. bukan karena gua ngerasa paling penting di dunia ini, tapi karena keberadaan gua sendiri udah cukup buat bikin ini jadi dunia gua. dunia ini tau, atau setidaknya keliatan sadar, kalau selama gua masih ada di sini, dunia gua masih berlaku.

soalnya logikanya gini aja: kalau suatu hari gua gak ada, ya ini otomatis berhenti jadi dunia gua. dunia gua gak bisa jalan tanpa gua. dunia gua cuma ada kalau gua ada. tapi sekarang gimana? sekarang gua ada. gua masih hidup di dunia gua, masih jalan di dunia gua, masih mikir hal2 gak penting di dunia gua, masih ngerasain capek, seneng, kecewa, overthinking, semua itu kejadian di dunia gua.

selama gua ada, dunia gua muter dari sudut pandang gua. apa yang gua liat, apa yang gua rasain, apa yang gua pikirin, semuanya ngebentuk dunia gua. mau dunia lain bilang apa, mau orang lain nganggep apa, itu urusan dunia mereka. dunia gua tetep dunia gua. karena faktanya gak ribet: selama gua masih napas, masih sadar, masih mikir, ini dunia gua.

dan selama gua belum pergi, selama gua masih ada di sini, dunia ini gak bisa pura2 jadi bukan dunia gua. mau dibantah kayak apa pun, mau dipelintir kayak apa pun, ujung-ujungnya tetep sama. karena gua ada, maka ini dunia gua. titik.

“This is all because of you.”
“I became human.”

Omg Eunho, who never wanted to become human, ends up becoming one, and it’s all because of Siyeol. Something about him causes her to lose her gumiho powers. Now it finally makes sense why Eunho is living in Siyeol’s house, and it also seems like Siyeol loses everything once Eunho’s powers disappear. That’s probably why the two of them are suddenly living such a modest life.

The story is getting more and more interesting. The conflict likely begins when Eunho loses her powers, which means Siyeol has to help her regain them. And somewhere along that journey, the resentment they once felt might slowly turn into love.

If you like gumiho stories, you should definitely watch No Tail To Tell, because like I said this one is different from typical gumiho stories.

LARAS HARUS BEBAS!!
Secara pribadi saya terus mengamati dan mengikuti bagaimana para aktivis demokrasi dikriminalisasi. Salah satunya Laras Faizati Khairunnisa ditangkap pada 1 September 2025 dan mulai ditahan sejak 2 September 2025 di Rutan Bareskrim Polri (kemudian dipindah ke tahanan jaksa dan rutan lain selama proses persidangan). 

Penahanan berlangsung lama (sekitar 4 bulan hingga akhir Desember 2025). Ia ditahan dengan dakwaan pasal pasal yg sanksi pidananya rata rata di atas 5 tahun. Inilah kedzoliman dan ketidaktepatan penerapan pasal yang dipakai menjerat terdakwa. Pasal-pasal yang dituduhkan (dakwaan berlapis dari jaksa):

Pasal 45A ayat (2) juncto Pasal 28 ayat (2) UU ITE (penyebaran informasi elektronik yang menimbulkan kebencian atau permusuhan berdasarkan SARA atau kelompok tertentu). Penggunaan pasal ini salah besar krn kerusuhan Agustus itu gak ada hubungannya dengan provokasi dan peristiwa SARA. Pakai pasal 28 ayat (2) ini sudah ngawur.

Kemudian penggunaan Pasal 48 ayat (1) juncto Pasal 32 ayat (1) atau (2) UU ITE, perubahan, perusakan, atau penyembunyian informasi/dokumen elektronik secara melawan hukum) ini juga salah. Pasal ini ditujukan larangan pada para hacker, bukan aktivis demokrasi. Tidak ada perbuatan mengubah informasi elektronik yg dilindungi UU ITE oleh aktivis demokrasi seperti Laras ini. Logis kalau akhirnya UU ITE tdk bisa dipakai dalam dakwaan dan tuntutan jaksa. Kasihan sekali mereka dikriminalisasi dengan penggunaan pasal pasal yg salah penerapan seperti ini.

Lalu ada lagi yang dipakai yaitu Pasal 160 KUHP (penghasutan secara lisan atau tulisan untuk melakukan perbuatan pidana atau melawan penguasa umum). Dan Pasal 161 ayat (1) KUHP (penyebaran tulisan/foto yang menghasut melakukan kekerasan atau perlawanan terhadap penguasa).

Tuduhan utama berasal dari unggahan Instagram Story Laras pada akhir Agustus 2025, saat demonstrasi berujung kerusuhan (termasuk kematian seorang ojol dilindas kendaraan polisi). Unggahan itu dianggap menghasut massa untuk membakar gedung Mabes Polri dan menyebarkan kebencian terhadap institusi Polri.

Ingat kerusuhan terjadi adalah setelah ada kematian Driver Ojol, Affan Kurniawan yg secara dramatis terlindas mobil rantis polisi. Jadi yg membuat marah demonstran itu ya kematian Affan ini. Artinya kerusuhan penyerangan kantor polisi itu dikarenakan adanya kematian driver ojol. Bukan karena provokasi konten dari aktivis.

Sementara pasal 161 KUHP itu adalah Delik Materiel, Jaksa harus membuktikan kebenaran bahwa munculnya kerusuhan itu benar2 dikarenakan tulisan atau ujaran terdakwa. Ini yang tidak masuk pasal yang didakwakan tersebut.

Cuma aparat sering asal pakai pasal pidana yang mendekati, walau sebenarnya norma larangannya tidak memenuhi unsur. Karena itu sudah sepantasnya hakim membebaskan Laras Faizati dan terdakwa lain yang kasusnya juga adanya kriminalisasi yang hampir sama, dengan penggunaan pasal pasal yg tidak tepat.

“The grotesque. Jung’s trickster isn’t “quirky chaos”; he is the primitive mismatch embodied, the place where boundaries fail—moral boundaries, conceptual boundaries, even bodily boundaries. Jung’s list of trickster traits is deliberately ugly: the trickster is “subhuman and superhuman, a bestial and divine being,” whose “chief and most alarming characteristic” is “his unconsciousness.”  He does “the most absurd or ‘unseemly’ deeds,” is dominated by “brute instincts,” and is “so naive” he can’t “see through” his own pranks.  And the crucial structural point: he is “a faithful reflection of an absolutely undifferentiated human consciousness” corresponding to a psyche that “has hardly left the animal level.”  Grotesque here means: the civilized frame keeps insisting on coherent personhood, while the trickster keeps demonstrating that personhood can be replaced by something more primitive and operational—an output-generator with appetites, reflexes, and opportunistic intelligence. That’s why the trickster’s “unseemliness” matters: it isn’t moral commentary, it’s the diagnostic sign that the figure is below the dignity-layer. The grotesque announces: “this is not the noble self speaking; this is the underside that civilization usually hides.” Jung underlines that the trickster is a collective shadow figure, “an epitome of all the inferior traits of character in individuals,” continually constructed out of what people disown.  And then he adds the blade that makes the “grotesque” decisive rather than decorative: the shadow can hide meaningful, even numinous contents “under an unprepossessing exterior.”  The ugly mask is not a contradiction; it is the delivery system. The low can carry the high.”

Mercurius in the Dock, or the Grotesque Operator Who Makes Logos Slip

This “dark craft” is old enough that it shows up as folklore long before philosophy tries to clean it into categories: a threshold-operator who wins by keeping meanings liquid, carrying power through the grotesque mask, and making the room’s closure-hunger do the work. The genealogy runs back to early literate worlds (3rd–2nd millennium BCE): Mesopotamian boundary-cunning and divine mediation motifs (the Enki/Ea type), Egyptian Thoth/Djehuty as scribe-magician and measurer of order (Old Kingdom onward, 3rd millennium BCE), then the Greek Hermes crystallizing the same function as lord of roads, gates, theft-by-cleverness, and message-routing (Archaic Greece, c. 8th–6th century BCE), and Roman Mercurius inheriting the exchange/conversion layer (Republic era cult, c. 3rd century BCE). In parallel, the same pattern lives in “folk” lineages: Norse Loki (preserved in medieval Icelandic texts, 13th century CE, but rooted in older oral myth), West African Anansi (Akan spider-trickster, oral for centuries, recorded heavily in the 18th–20th centuries), and Indigenous North American tricksters like Coyote/Raven (pre-contact oral systems, later written down mainly in the 19th–20th centuries). South Asian tradition carries its own versions—Narada as divine instigator/messenger in epic–Puranic cycles, and Krishna’s strategic, reality-bending counsel in the Mahābhārata tradition (composition layers roughly late 1st millennium BCE into early centuries CE). Jung’s 20th-century “trickster” paper is basically a modern psychological reading of this same cross-cultural engine: the subhuman/superhuman mismatch, the obscene and holy braided together, the grotesque vessel carrying a cold intelligence that doesn’t argue a world into being so much as keeps the hinge open until the world retypes itself.

I could not have said this anywhere near so well.  I, all too often these last few years, find myself more ready to use the sword than my words.  It takes a conscious and considerable effort to stay my hand and be kind at times.  Thank you for the reminder that we are not alone in our struggles.

May your Christmas be blessed.

Listening to an acoustic ambient album I love that is perfect for the season and ruminating on the year in the short moment of peace I have between the closing of the day, the emptying of the shop, and my ritual run by the store on the way home to get about the blessed duties of husband & father.

I will definitely be on X between now and the end of the year. That said, this felt to be the right moment to say THANK YOU to all of you who have helped grow my businesses account here on X. I could go on about how much money we have invested in this project and for how far we have to go to have made it all financially sensible, however, I know that none of this would be happening without you all. I am tired so I will not go on at length. Just know, that you are all encouraging and a joy. I am truly thankful. All of us ar Harvey's are thankful.

I want to share this, also. It is not always easy to tell when someone is struggling, down, or outright overwhelmed, and yet, often, that seemingly errant encouraging word reaches you, through X no less, and it meets you right when you need it. I have experienced this, personally, and I know others have also.

This can be every bit a wicked place. Yet, like all things that exist or have ever been made, nothing is beyond redemption. Nothing can escape the workings of redemption, even if only contrasting the good. 

So, consider a tender heart. Try it for a season. When you feel the venom in you for a biting remark, or the defensiveness well up, or even a lewd joke or barb, consider encouragement. Consider wisdom. Consider light over dark. 

We are all stumbling towards something, be it good or ill. This Christmas season consider that we might choose to stumble towards the good. Consider that what others do not know or understand about you that you, in turn, do not understand about them. Consider that your words, that our words, do have power, and as we are responsible for every round that leaves the muzzle of our gun so are we responsible for every word that leaves our tongue. This is not a call to permissiveness, heaven knows we've had a cauldron full of that. No, this is a call to doing the work of discernment before we speak. A sword in one hand and love in the other. Which are you more ready to wield?

Okay. There will be more from me but I will leave you with that.

I appreciate you all deeply. Maybe. Maybe there is good to be mined out of this dark world after all. Let us hope for that. 

Merry Christmas to all. Praise God for a Savior was born and He Is.

Be well my friends.
